commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e0e10b389dc3c08b27890db5d78e47d3fc9ef58f)
commands.review_summary:
Adds CLI-level hard timeout handling for `openclaw agent --local` and embedded fallback runs, plus focused tests and `docs/cli/agent.md` timeout documentation.

PR surface: Source +219, Tests +314, Docs 0. Total +533 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main awaits local and fallback `agentCommand` promises directly, so a non-settling embedded run can keep the CLI alive. There is not yet high-confidence live exact-head proof from a real wedged local agent command.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer compatibility judgment plus contributor live proof; there is no safe automated repair path while the PR remains draft and proof-gated.

target.title: fix(cli): enforce local agent hard timeout
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw agent --local --timeout N` can still leave the CLI alive until an outer wrapper timeout kills it. The lower-level embedded agent timeout is useful, but it is not a command-level wall-clock guarantee: setup, retries, cleanup, or a wedged embedded run can still keep the CLI process around after the operator-specified timeout window.

## Change and value

This PR adds a CLI-level hard timeout for embedded local agent runs. Explicit `--local` runs and embedded fallback runs now share a wall-clock boundary of the resolved `--timeout N` plus 30 seconds of grace. If that timer fires, the CLI aborts the local run signal and exits with code `124`, matching the exit code operators already see from GNU `timeout` wrappers.

The value is that scripts no longer need to wait for their own much larger timeout window just because the embedded run failed to settle cleanly.

## Who is affected, and who is not

- Affected: operators and automation running `openclaw agent --local` with a finite `--timeout` or configured agent timeout.
- Affected: gateway-backed runs only after they enter embedded fallback; the normal Gateway request path keeps its existing timeout and `chat.abort` behavior.
- Not affected: `--timeout 0`; that continues to mean no CLI hard timeout.
- Not fixed here: post-success event-loop handles that keep the process alive after a successful local result. That is covered separately by #86264.

## Why now

Local diagnostics showed the second CLI-exit class directly: an `agent --local` run can fail to produce a final result before the outer wrapper kills it, even when the inner `--timeout` is shorter. This patch makes the local CLI boundary own that wall-clock guarantee instead of relying only on the embedded runner to unwind itself.

## Implementation

- Creates a bounded embedded-run helper around the existing `agentCommand` call sites.
- Uses `--timeout N + 30s` as the hard deadline, with `--timeout 0` disabling the timer.
- Forwards the resolved timeout into `agentCommand`, so cooperative embedded abort and the outer hard boundary agree on the same timeout.
- Preserves `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM` precedence: signals still exit promptly with `130`/`143`, even if the embedded run ignores abort or rejects during teardown.
- On timeout, aborts the signal, writes a concise timeout error, and exits through `runtime.exit(124)`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw agent --local --timeout N` and embedded fallback runs should have a command-level wall-clock timeout when the local run ignores or fails to settle after lower-level aborts.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux/WSL from `openclaw/openclaw` main at `4798264a2993412a26209d6f1abee9cd83b5c0e6`, branch head `8790ec43051f30fb48a5b8772ff4f3671f9307cd2`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=10000
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts
pnpm check:docs
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

Evidence after fix: The affected command test file passed: `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts` 54/54. The new regressions cover:

- local agent promises that never settle, exiting `124` at `--timeout + 30s`
- default omitted `--timeout` resolving to `600` and being forwarded into the embedded runner
- `--timeout 0` not arming the hard timer
- successful local runs clearing the hard timer
- `SIGTERM` winning over ignored local aborts and teardown rejection
- embedded fallback runs receiving the same hard boundary

Observed result after fix: The modeled local CLI timeout path now exits at the command boundary instead of waiting indefinitely for the local agent promise to settle.

What was not tested: A real live `openclaw agent --local --timeout N` run that wedges and exits with 124 from this branch. This PR is draft until maintainers accept this policy shape or we add live exact-head proof.

## Verification

- `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts`: 54/54 passed under `test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Codex review: no discrete actionable correctness regressions on this patch after the timeout-forwarding fix
- Claude diff review: no blocking defects found
- Read-only lane reviews found and drove fixes for signal precedence, timer ref behavior, fallback coverage, and timeout propagation

## Relationship to #86264

#86264 handles the post-completion cleanup class: a command has finished but deferred context-engine maintenance keeps work alive. This PR handles the no-completion class: a local/embedded agent command never settles, so the CLI needs a hard wall-clock boundary.

AI-assisted: yes.
target.author: kaspre
